BLEU Cape Town
BLEU› Sleep› Cape Town
◈ Anxiety◉ Gut Health◐ Sleep

Cape Town is the most beautiful city in Africa. It is also one of the most unequal in the world. Both things are true and both take a toll.

Elevated nighttime cortisol is preventing your nervous system from downshifting into sleep.

Local data — Cape Town Cape Town's inequality index is among the highest of any major global city.
The street truth

The load-shedding alone — up to twelve hours a day without power — creates a chronic stress pattern that has no equivalent elsewhere. The inequality is not invisible. The violence anxiety is not paranoia. The beauty is real and so is everything underneath it.

What it feels like
  • Waking between 2–4am, unable to fall back asleep
  • Mind racing the moment your head hits the pillow
  • Exhausted after 7–8 hours — like you never slept
  • Depending on something to get to sleep and something to get through the day
Root cause analysis
  • Cortisol rhythm misalignment — cortisol stays elevated when it should be falling
  • Inadequate parasympathetic activation before the sleep window
  • Nervous system locked in fight-or-flight at bedtime
Primary driver in Cape Town
Economic inequality anxiety + load-shedding chronic stress + inequality visibility gut stress.
Across Cape Town
From the CBD to Khayelitsha, from the Waterfront to Mitchells Plain — the mountain watches it all.
The honest ask

The resilience of this city is real. This protocol is for sustaining it.

Start Sleep Reset Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3)
8:00 PM
💡
Dim all lights to 10%
Start melatonin signal 2hrs early
9:00 PM
💊
Take Magnesium Glycinate 400mg
Allow 90 min to reach therapeutic level
9:30 PM
📵
No screens — all devices down
Blue light suppresses melatonin 50%
9:45 PM
🎧
Play Delta Sleep Track
0.5–4 Hz · Entrains deep sleep architecture
10:00 PM
🫁
Box breathing — 4 rounds
4 in · 4 hold · 4 out · 4 hold · activates vagus nerve
10:30 PM
🌙
Lights out
Do not check the time. Trust the system.

0 / 6 steps

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Sleep Reset Protocol. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU's outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

Cortisol and Sleep Architecture
Elevated evening cortisol delays sleep onset by an average of 47 minutes and reduces slow-wave sleep by 23%.
Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 2019
Magnesium Glycinate and Sleep Quality
400mg magnesium glycinate supplementation improved sleep efficiency by 19% in adults with insomnia in a 8-week RCT.
Journal of Research in Medical Sciences, 2012
Blue Light and Melatonin Suppression
Blue light exposure before bed suppresses melatonin production by 50% and shifts the circadian clock by up to 3 hours.
Harvard Medical School, 2015
Ashwagandha and Cortisol Reduction
KSM-66 ashwagandha at 600mg/day reduced serum cortisol levels by 27.9% over 60 days compared to placebo.
Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine, 2012
L-Theanine and Sleep Quality
L-theanine (200mg) significantly improved sleep quality scores and reduced sleep disturbance in a double-blind crossover study.
Asia Pacific Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2008
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every check-in from every user in Cape Town becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in Cape Town — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community it serves.

Your journal trains your personal protocol. Every entry is a data point. Every data point makes the next recommendation more precise. This is how BLEU gets better for you specifically — not for everyone in general.

Every entry you make in Cape Town becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in Cape Town log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here.
BLEU · ALVAI · Cape Town

ALVAI knows your city. It knows the signal — the beauty, inequality, and constant uncertainty. It knows the condition. Ask it anything about your sleep protocol. It cross-references 7 federal health databases, 302,000 drug interaction pairs, and Jazz Bird's city-level intelligence in real time.

◐
ALVAI active for Cape Town — Sleep protocol. I know the beauty, inequality, and constant uncertainty. I know what that does to the body. Ask me anything.
Common questions from Cape Town
ALVAI provides information, not medical advice. Drug interactions are flagged for review, not prescribed. Dr. Felicia Stoler audits the knowledge infrastructure.
Jazz Bird Network — Cape Town

The people using BLEU in Cape Town are building the city's health intelligence without knowing each other. Their outcomes — anonymized, never sold — compound into the protocol improvements everyone here benefits from.

The Hand-Holding Network
Nobody in Cape Town knows anyone else using BLEU. Their data never touches. But when 166 people in Cape Town report that week 2 is the hardest — the system tells the next person before they get there. That's the hand-holding network. Invisible. Compounding. Yours.
